summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Left hand pain

History of Present Illness
The patient reports pain in the left hand. The patient recommends cutting back on spicy foods. The doctor recommends cutting back on caffeine. The doctor recommends cutting back on alcohol. The patient has a history of reflux. The patient's reflux was managed. The patient was consuming alcohol. The patient denies shortness of breath. The patient denies chest pain. The patient denies belly pain. The patient reports no symptoms. The patient feels comfortable. The patient's attitude improved. The patient denies nausea. The patient had gastritis.

Findings
The patient has 1+ pitting edema bilaterally in the lower extremities. The patient has a slight 2/6 systolic ejection murmur. The patient has a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur. The patient has 1+ pitting edema bilaterally.

Assessment
The patient's symptoms are consistent with a complex clinical picture requiring further evaluation.

Plan
The doctor recommends lifestyle modifications. The doctor wants the patient to continue protonix 40 mg a day. The doctor prescribed protonix. The patient moved over from caffeine to green tea.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Follow-up chronic problems.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Alexander Myers is a 62-year-old male with a past medical history significant for reflux who presents for follow-up of his chronic problems.

In regards to his reflux, he reports that he has continued to utilize Protonix 40 mg daily. He states this has helped a lot and he is really comfortable. The patient is doing really well with his diet. He reports that he has discontinued drinking energy drinks and is now drinking green tea. The patient states this change has also helped reduce his side effects.

He notes that his reflux being improved has helped his mood. The patient has a good support system at home and all of his children call to check on him daily. He also notes his job is going well.

The patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

• Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain or dyspnea on exertion.
• Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

• Neurological/Psychological: Appropriate mood and affect.
• Neck: No carotid bruits appreciable.
• Respiratory: Lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
• Cardiovascular: Regular rate. Slight 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, stable.
• Musculoskeletal: 1+ pitting edema bilaterally in the lower extremities.

RESULTS

Endoscopy from last year revealed mild gastritis.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Alexander Myers is a 62-year-old male with a past medical history significant for acid reflux. He presents today for follow up of his chronic problems.

Acid reflux.
• Medical Reasoning: This is well controlled on Protonix and dietary modifications.
• Medical Treatment: Continue Protonix 40 mg daily.
• Patient Education and Counseling: I advised the patient to continue with lifestyle and dietary modifications.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Left hand pain
History of Present Illness
The patient reports pain in the left hand. The patient recommends cutting back on spicy foods. The doctor recommends cutting back on caffeine. The doctor recommends cutting back on alcohol. section_miscategorizations.text: The patient recommends cutting back on spicy foods.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a recommendation/intervention (even if oddly attributed to the patient) and functions as part of the management plan rather than history of present illness.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor recommends cutting back on caffeine.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a clinician recommendation and belongs in the Plan section, not in the symptom/history narrative.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor recommends cutting back on alcohol.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a lifestyle counseling/management instruction and should be documented in the Plan section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient moved over from caffeine to green tea.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This describes a change the patient has already implemented (past behavior/history) rather than a new order or instruction, so it fits better in the history narrative, though its placement in Plan is not highly misleading.
